_comment: Example filled profile (fictional org) — a reference for writing your own. NOT loaded by setup; onboarding writes the real profile. Shows how the fields generalize beyond any one org.
one_liner: a nonprofit building open-source environmental sensors for rural communities
mission: Put affordable, trustworthy air- and water-quality data in the hands of communities that regulators overlook.
problem: Rural and low-income communities lack the monitoring infrastructure to detect pollution, so harms go undocumented and unaddressed.
stage: early operating — two deployed pilots, seeking to scale hardware + data platform
team_summary: four staff: two hardware engineers, one community organizer, one data scientist
traction: 2 county pilots live, ~40 sensors deployed, one published dataset; no earned revenue
revenue_model: grants + optional data-platform support contracts with municipalities
capabilities: low-cost IoT sensor design and manufacturing
open data pipelines and public dashboards
ethos: Open by default, community-owned data, and rigor over hype. We prioritize funders who respect community sovereignty over data and do not require exclusive IP.

eligibility_constraints.label: Entity
eligibility_constraints.detail: 501(c)(3) nonprofit — eligible for most foundation and government grants; for corporate/for-profit-only funds, check for a nonprofit track.

eligibility_constraints.label: Affiliation
eligibility_constraints.detail: No university affiliation. Flag grants that require an academic PI unless a partner path exists.

eligibility_constraints.label: Geography
eligibility_constraints.detail: US-based, Oregon-focused but can serve any US community. For place-based funders, check whether our region qualifies.

eligibility_constraints.label: Open source
eligibility_constraints.detail: Our hardware and data are open by default. Flag any grant that requires exclusive/proprietary outputs — likely a mismatch.

open_source_posture: outputs are open by default; proprietary-output requirements are usually a disqualifier

framing_angles.name: Environmental justice
framing_angles.description: monitoring for communities that regulators overlook; documenting undocumented harms

framing_angles.name: Open science infrastructure
framing_angles.description: open hardware, open data pipelines, reproducible community monitoring

framing_angles.name: Civic technology
framing_angles.description: public dashboards and participatory data for local decision-making

target_grant_types: environmental foundations funding monitoring, air/water quality, or environmental justice
government (EPA, state) community-monitoring and citizen-science programs
place-based funders serving the Pacific Northwest
calibration_notes: Strong fits: environmental-justice and open-science funders that accept nonprofit applicants and value community data ownership. Stretch-but-real: general climate/tech funders where we frame as open infrastructure. Fast-pass: funders requiring an academic PI, proprietary IP, or paying customers.
